headline: Engineering leader who's shipped 6 products to 100K+ users — interested in scaling payments infrastructure at Stripe
about: I build products that handle money and trust at scale. Over the past 4 years I've shipped 6 products across fintech, AI, and mobile — from a UPI checkout handling ₹2Cr+ TPV across 6 broker integrations to an AI plant-care app serving 100K+ users. I think like a PM, ship like an engineer, and obsess over the details that make users trust a product with their money. I'm looking for a role where I can own an ambitious problem end-to-end at a company whose infrastructure I've already studied closely.

why_im_a_fit.bullet: Shipped UPI checkout flow for Wealth Sage handling ₹2Cr+ TPV across 6 brokers — directly maps to 'scaling payments infrastructure' in the JD.
6 products shipped (Plant Sage, Kahaani, Sage-Bot, Wealth Sage). PM mindset, ships full-stack. Maps to 'owns the roadmap end-to-end.'
Led teams across 3 timezones (Hyderabad + SF + London). Stripe India team needs this.

work.title: Founder & Engineering Lead
bullets: Built UPI checkout + Zero Touch Command GTT trading. ₹2Cr+ TPV, 6 broker integrations.
Reduced repeat-checkout drop-off by 4.1% with single-screen UPI intent pattern.
Shipped n8n-powered automation layer eliminating 6hrs/week of manual reconciliation.

work.title: Founder & Product Lead
bullets: AI plant care for 100K+ users. Built and shipped Android app from 0 to 1.
Integrated Claude + OpenRouter multi-model routing for diagnosis flows.
Maintained 4.6-star rating with proactive push notification personalization.

work.title: Engineering Manager
bullets: Led cross-functional team of 8 across design, mobile, and backend.
Delivered 3 product launches on time; reduced release cycle from 3 weeks to 5 days.
Introduced structured retrospectives that cut bug escape rate by 40%.

pitch_section.title: What I'd ship at Stripe
pitch_section.problem: I tested Checkout's India flow end-to-end as a returning user with a saved UPI method. On the first screen, Stripe correctly shows the saved VPA — good. But on tap, it routes to a fresh 'enter UPI ID' screen instead of triggering the saved-method intent. This adds 2 taps (re-enter VPA, re-confirm), which our research at Wealth Sage found drops conversion 8–12% on returning checkout.
pitch_section.hypothesis: A single-screen 'Use saved · pay ₹X' intent with a 5-second timeout fallback will recover 4–6% of returning-user checkout drop-off without adding auth risk.
pitch_section.proposed_solution: Ship a single-screen confirm pattern: detect saved VPA, pre-fill amount, show 'Confirm ▶' as primary CTA with 5s auto-dismiss fallback to manual entry. I shipped exactly this pattern at Wealth Sage in March 2026 and recovered 4.1% on repeat-checkout.
metrics_to_track: UPI intent success rate vs. manual re-entry rate
Fallback trigger rate (how often 5s timeout fires)
tradeoffs: Slightly higher implementation complexity for the intent pre-fill layer
Requires maintaining saved-method sync with NPCI VPA state
May not generalize to first-time users (scope: returning users only)
guardrails: No change to fraud detection or auth flows
Fallback to manual entry if VPA validation fails
A/B test on 10% of returning India users before full rollout
Respect existing payment method expiry / revocation signals

evidence.caption: Current behavior: re-prompt on saved VPA tap
